title: launch eva somehow
wikitext:
[]: launch eva... somehow             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Sep 22, 2009 12:54 pm I'm just wondering how exactly the evas travel from below Nerv HQ to Tokyo-3 seemingly by going straight up when there seems to be a pretty obvious gaping hole between the two locations. Any ideas?

[]:             Quote Post by VoidEater » Tue Sep 22, 2009 1:02 pm Don't at least some of the schematics of their travel path have significant angles? If they are sufficiently underground below the pyramid, say, they can go up for some time prior to skating along the geofront's sphere.

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Tue Sep 22, 2009 1:05 pm The maps of the Eva launch pads show that there's lots of twists and turns. We just never get to see them.

[]:             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Tue Sep 22, 2009 1:12 pm If Rebuild was any indication, the system appears to work in conjuction with the giant elevator that moved Misato's car to-and-fro. SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Sep 22, 2009 1:29 pm The last time this was discussed there wasn't much to say other than "maybe they don't go straight up, despite what we're led to believe".

[]:             Quote Post by Holy Diver » Tue Sep 22, 2009 3:30 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: The last time this was discussed there wasn't much to say other than "maybe they don't go straight up, despite what we're led to believe".

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Sep 22, 2009 3:46 pm Holy Diver wrote: This is just one of many different problems in Eva that deserve some good old fashioned fanwanking.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Sep 22, 2009 6:03 pm eh, was hoping there actually was some attempt at an explanation somewhere along the line. oh well.

[]:             Quote Post by Mongoosedog » Tue Sep 22, 2009 6:22 pm i know in one episode it shows on a computer screen the eva's travelling diagonally upward as well using a little diagram thing

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Sep 22, 2009 7:13 pm Sigh. Let's just say that evas launch via Wankavator. [insert mental image of Gendo surrounded by a choir of gloomily chanting ganguro Rei-tachi as he parades the other pilot candidates through his nightmare factory.]

[]:             Quote Post by MatrixRefugee » Tue Sep 22, 2009 7:54 pm Holy Diver wrote: The Eva Monkey wrote: The last time this was discussed there wasn't much to say other than "maybe they don't go straight up, despite what we're led to believe".

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Wed Sep 23, 2009 7:17 am There actually was a shot in the first 2 episodes of the TV series where you see a computer screen with all the tunnels, and a white dot indicating the Eva 01's current position within those tunnels. The tunnels all had "forks in the road" within them, creating a pyramid grid structure, and you saw the Eva 01 take one of these forks to change direction and end up in the proper location to do battle. The shot is only about a second long, hence very few people remembering it.

[]:             Quote Post by Cypher » Wed Sep 23, 2009 7:32 am Well the eva's are moved from the cage to the launch tubes so they could be in a completely different part of the facility by then and be launched straight up.

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Wed Sep 23, 2009 7:37 am Wormhole.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Sep 23, 2009 8:08 am The chart people keep referring to can't possibly be to scale, especially as the various terminal points on the launching system end in both the geofront, the downtown area of the city, and the distant edges of town. It's just a simplified flowchart of pathways and gates, not an actual map.

[]:             Quote Post by Sharaz Destler » Wed Sep 23, 2009 10:14 am NemZ wrote: The chart people keep referring to can't possibly be to scale, especially as the various terminal points on the launching system end in both the geofront, the downtown area of the city, and the distant edges of town.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Sep 23, 2009 11:38 am THEY USE ANGLES AND SHIT, DAWG.
